step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the assessment that peau d'orange in breast cancer is mainly due to obstruction of sub-dermal lymphatics (Option A). raw_response:
I agree with the assessment that peau d'orange in breast cancer is mainly due to obstruction of sub-dermal lymphatics (Option A). The rationale provided accurately describes how this obstruction leads to local edema and changes in skin texture, mimicking an orange peel. There isn't any compelling evidence suggesting infiltration into Cooper’s ligaments would cause such a specific dermatological sign as opposed to other symptoms like dimpling or retraction.

Answer: A.
